prose: Prochloraz returns a negative call at adversarial_negative tier because the curated mechanistic layer carries no diagnostic signal: there is no curated Parkinson's DirectEvidence and no registration as a neuro-AOP stressor [E1], and no AOP pathway is attached. This absence—not any suppression of activity—drives the call. The compound is genuinely bioactive, with 16 mechanistic assay hits including 3 mitochondrial flags [E2], precisely the kind of promiscuous signal that superficially resembles a neurodegeneration hypothesis. But assay bioactivity is corroboration only and anti-diagnostic; it cannot substitute for a curated mechanistic anchor, so the engine correctly declines to act on it. Brain-penetrance is plausible [E3], yet penetrance alone discriminates nothing absent an upstream mechanism to deliver. The evidence-ranked mechanistic hypothesis is therefore null: no causal claim is made, and the bioactivity is best read as an imposter pattern, not a suppressed positive.

citations.detail: No curated Parkinson's DirectEvidence and not a registered neuro-AOP stressor.
citations.reference: Davis AP, Wiegers TC, Johnson RJ, et al. Comparative Toxicogenomics Database (CTD): update 2023. Nucleic Acids Research. 2023;51(D1):D1257-D1262. Mechanistic scaffold: OECD Adverse Outcome Pathway Wiki. AOP 3: Inhibition of the mitochondrial complex I of nigro-striatal neurons leads to parkinsonian motor deficits (WPHA/WNT Endorsed).

citations.detail: Bioactive: 16 mechanistic assay hits (incl. 3 mitochondrial). This is the imposter signal the engine correctly does NOT act on.
citations.reference: U.S. EPA ToxCast/Tox21 invitroDB (v4.2), accessed through the NICEATM Integrated Chemical Environment (ICE). Active hitcalls only; queried on the salt-form-correct record.

citations.detail: Brain-penetrant, but penetrance is not a discriminator here.
citations.reference: Meng F, Xi Y, Huang J, Ayers PW. A curated diverse molecular database of blood-brain barrier permeability with chemical descriptors (B3DB). Scientific Data. 2021;8:289. Daina A, Zoete V. A BOILED-Egg to predict gastrointestinal absorption and brain access of small molecules. ChemMedChem. 2016;11(11):1117-1121.
